intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely inaccurate regarding the current state of the biome. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not identifiable as healthy specimens. P3 (Pothos) is the only identifiable plant, though it is in a state of severe decline.

inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (No visible silver guest foliage)

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot; soil appears dry and undisturbed.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains no visible mint; soil surface is dark and potentially damp.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos leaf is present but shows significant chlorosis and loss of turgor pressure. The white rabbit anchor is present.
plant_audit.P4: Merged with P2; no distinct silver guest foliage detected.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, suggesting high moisture content or peat-heavy substrate.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P3/P4 pot, acting as a calcium supplement or mulch.
biome_observations.anomalies: The lighting environment has shifted to a low-intensity blue/UV spectrum in the final frames, obscuring biological detail.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Initial state shows a single, struggling Pothos leaf and eggshell debris. Soil remains dark.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: Severe light degradation. The scene transitions to near-total darkness, rendering visual assessment of plant health impossible in the final frame.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of critical failure. The Pothos (P3) is the only remaining specimen, and its posture indicates severe dehydration or root rot. The lack of growth in P1, P2, and P4 suggests these specimens have perished.
vision_report.anomalies: Introduction of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment. Significant lighting failure in the final sequence.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical collection is suffering from a lack of viable light and likely improper soil moisture management. The Pothos is the sole survivor of a likely collapse of the other three specimens. The transition to a blue-spectrum light source in the final images suggests a technical failure or an attempt to force growth that is currently ineffective.
